---
title: "Alport Glom Dataset ShinyCell Upload"
author: "Jonathan Nelson"
date: "`r format(Sys.time(), '%d %B %Y')`"
output:
  html_document: 
    toc: yes
    toc_depth: 3
    toc_float: true
    number_sections: yes
    theme: bootstrap
    df_print: paged
    code_folding: hide
    highlight: pygments
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```


```{r load new packages, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE, error=FALSE, message=FALSE}

if (!require("dplyr")) {install.packages("dplyr"); require("dplyr")}
if (!require("Seurat")) {install.packages("Seurat"); require("Seurat")}
if (!require("patchwork")) {install.packages("patchwork"); require("patchwork")}
if (!require("knitr")) {install.packages("knitr"); require("knitr")}
if (!require("ggplot2")) {install.packages("ggplot2"); require("ggplot2")}
if (!require("BiocManager")) {install.packages("BiocManager"); require("BiocManager")}
if (!require("tibble")) {install.packages("tibble"); require("tibble")}
if (!require("ggpmisc")) {install.packages("ggpmisc"); require("ggpmisc")}
if (!require("RColorBrewer")) {install.packages("RColorBrewer"); require("RColorBrewer")} #color
if (!require("ggrepel")) {install.packages("ggrepel"); require("ggrepel")}
if (!require("DESeq2")) {BiocManager::install('DESeq2'); require("DESeq2")}
if (!require("here")) {install.packages("here"); require("here")}
if (!require("Signac")) {BiocManager::install('Signac'); require("Signac")}

here()

```


# Load Data


```{r load data, echo=TRUE, warning=FALSE, error=FALSE, message=FALSE}

SO_all <- readRDS(here("outputs", "Alports_041525.rds"))

SO_all@meta.data <- SO_all@meta.data %>% 
  mutate(class_poster_name = dplyr::case_when(
    class_merge == "EC"  ~ "Endothelial Cell",
    class_merge == "IMM"  ~ "Immune Cell",
    class_merge == "MES"  ~ "Mesangial Cell",
    class_merge == "PODO"  ~ "Podocyte",
    class_merge == "PEC"  ~ "Parietal Epithelial Cell",
    class_merge == "VSMC"  ~ "Vascular Smooth Muscle Cell"
    ))

SO_all@meta.data$class_poster_name <- factor(SO_all@meta.data$class_poster_name, levels = c("Endothelial Cell", "Immune Cell", "Mesangial Cell", "Podocyte", "Parietal Epithelial Cell", "Vascular Smooth Muscle Cell"))

DimPlot(SO_all, group.by = "class_poster_name") + 
  ylab("UMAP 2") +
  xlab("UMAP 1") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 20),
    axis.line = element_line(size = 1, colour = "black"),
    axis.text = element_blank(),          # Remove axis text
    axis.ticks = element_blank(),         # Optional: Remove axis ticks
    text = element_text(size = 20)
  ) + 
  ggtitle("")

```


```{r echo=TRUE, warning=FALSE, error=FALSE, message=FALSE, fig.align = 'center'}

################### 1. ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Shinycell installation
reqPkg = c("data.table", "Matrix", "hdf5r", "reticulate", "ggplot2", 
           "gridExtra", "glue", "readr", "RColorBrewer", "R.utils", "Seurat")
newPkg = reqPkg[!(reqPkg %in% installed.packages()[,"Package"])]
if(length(newPkg)){install.packages(newPkg)}

# If you are using h5ad file as input, run the code below as well
# reticulate::py_install("anndata")

#devtools::install_github("SGDDNB/ShinyCell")


################### 2. Skip if the seurat object is ready to be loaded directly. --------------------------------------------------

seu <- SO_all

head(seu@meta.data)

#Confirm active identity of dataset

Idents(seu) <- seu@meta.data$class_poster_name

head(seu@active.ident)

# The down-size step is redundant - as ShinyCell does this automatically. Keeping the following lines here just for the record.
# Down-size the seurat object by removing the raw counts, keeping the scaled.data, and keeping one assay --------------------------
# seu <- DietSeurat(seu, counts = FALSE, scale.data = TRUE, dimreducs = "umap", assays = "RNA")

# Below is the DietSeurat() function details.
# DietSeurat(
#   object,
#   counts = TRUE, # Preserve the count matrices for the assays specified
#   data = TRUE, # Preserve the data slot for the assays specified
#   scale.data = FALSE, # Preserve the scale.data slot for the assays specified
#   features = NULL, # Only keep a subset of features, defaults to all features
#   assays = NULL, # Only keep a subset of assays specified here
#   dimreducs = NULL, # Only keep a subset of DimReducs specified here (if NULL, remove all DimReducs)
#   graphs = NULL, # Only keep a subset of Graphs specified here (if NULL, remove all Graphs)
#   misc = TRUE # Preserve the misc slot; default is TRUE
# )

# Clean up the meta.data for more user-friendly interface (not required) ----------------------------------------------------------

head(seu@meta.data)

seu$orig.ident <- NULL
seu$unintegrated_clusters <- NULL
seu$seurat_clusters<- NULL
seu$RNA_snn_res.0.4 <- NULL
seu$pANN <- NULL
seu$SCT_snn_res.1 <- NULL
seu$subclass_merge <- NULL
seu$group_num <- NULL
seu$SCT_snn_res.2 <- NULL
seu$S.Score <- NULL
seu$G2M.Score <- NULL
seu$DF.class <- NULL
seu$class_merge <- NULL


head(seu@meta.data)

# Rename the columns for easier understanding

colnames(seu@meta.data)[colnames(seu@meta.data) == "CellType"] ="Cell Type"
colnames(seu@meta.data)[colnames(seu@meta.data) == "percent.mt"] ="Percent Mitochondria"
colnames(seu@meta.data)[colnames(seu@meta.data) == "sample"] ="Sample"
colnames(seu@meta.data)[colnames(seu@meta.data) == "batch"] ="Batch"
colnames(seu@meta.data)[colnames(seu@meta.data) == "group"] ="Group"
colnames(seu@meta.data)[colnames(seu@meta.data) == "alports"] ="Alport"
colnames(seu@meta.data)[colnames(seu@meta.data) == "ev"] ="EV"
colnames(seu@meta.data)[colnames(seu@meta.data) == "old.ident"] ="Abbreviated Cell Type"
colnames(seu@meta.data)[colnames(seu@meta.data) == "class_poster_name"] ="Cell Type"

# Reorder the columns from important ones as the order will be consistent with the drop-down menu

colnames(seu@meta.data) # Get all column names

# [1] "nCount_RNA"            "nFeature_RNA"          "Percent Mitochondria"  "Sample"               
# [5] "nCount_SCT"            "nFeature_SCT"          "Batch"                 "Group"                
# [9] "Alports"               "EV"                    "Phase"                 "Abbreviated Cell Type"
# [13] "Cell Type"    

# Re-order the columns by name

seu@meta.data <- seu@meta.data[, c( "Cell Type", "Group", "Alport", "EV", "Phase", "Sample", "Batch", "Abbreviated Cell Type", "nCount_RNA", "nFeature_RNA", "nCount_SCT", "nFeature_SCT", "Percent Mitochondria"), ]            

colnames(seu@meta.data) # Get all column names

# [1] "Cell Type"             "Group"                 "Alports"               "EV"                   
# [5] "Phase"                 "Sample"                "Batch"                 "Abbreviated Cell Type"
# [9] "nCount_RNA"            "nFeature_RNA"          "nCount_SCT"            "nFeature_SCT"         
# [13] "Percent Mitochondria" 

################### 3.-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Generate the shiny app

library(Seurat)
library(ShinyCell)

scConf = createConfig(seu)
makeShinyApp(seu, scConf, gene.mapping = TRUE, gex.assay = "SCT", default.gene1 = "Flt1", default.gene2 = "Nphs2",
             shiny.title = "scRNAseq of Glomerular Cells from Mice with Alport Syndrome",
       default.multigene = c( "Flt1",        # Endo
                      "Ptprc",        # Immune
                      "Pdgfrb",       # Mesangial
                      "Nphs2",        # Podo        
                      "Ncam1",        # PEC
                      "Acta2"        # VSMC
                            )   
             ) 

```

```{r, echo=TRUE, warning=FALSE, error=FALSE, message=FALSE, fig.align = 'center'}


default.multigene <- c( "Flt1",        # Endo
                      "Ptprc",        # Immune
                      "Pdgfrb",       # Mesangial
                      "Nphs2",        # Podo        
                      "Ncam1",        # PEC
                      "Acta2"        # VSMC
                            )    

DotPlot(seu, features = default.multigene)
```


# Session Info

```{r sessioninfo, echo=TRUE, warning=FALSE, error=FALSE, message=FALSE, fig.align = 'center'}

sessionInfo()

```
